conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world: Given the function f(x)=x³ - 2x + 1, what is the derivative value f'(2) of this function at x=2?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world, given the function expression, find the derivative value</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use conventional derivative calculation, f'(x)=3x² - 2, f'(2)=3×4 - 2 = 12 - 2 = 10.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for derivatives under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, f'(x)=3x² - 2, f'(2)=10.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
